frameworks.name: causal chain of effects in NIBS
frameworks.one_line: stimulation generates E-field, modulates local and network activity, then cognition

frameworks.name: correlative neuroimaging
frameworks.one_line: purely observational measures lacking causal inferential power

frameworks.name: experimental sensory stimulation
frameworks.one_line: input-side manipulation cannot localize causation in brain tissue

core_question: Under what conditions do noninvasive brain stimulation experiments warrant genuinely causal claims about cognition?
key_assumptions: NIBS effects propagate through a multi-step causal chain (E-field, neural activity, network spread, cognition)
each link in the chain can be confounded if not explicitly tested
appropriate control conditions can isolate intended causal effects
manipulations: review-level discussion of stimulation modality (TMS, tDCS, tACS) and target region
consideration of control conditions for each link in the causal chain
key_findings: research hypotheses in NIBS studies often oversimplify the underlying causal chain
each link (field generation, local activity, network propagation, cognitive effect) carries distinct confounds requiring tailored controls
interpretations: NIBS can establish cause-effect relationships only when assumptions and confounds at every link are explicitly tested and controlled
without such controls, causal language in NIBS findings is unwarranted
